# anti-hall

> A Claude Code marketplace plugin plus a Codex-native port that makes coding agents verify before they claim.
> It injects a verify-first protocol at session start (re-firing after compaction), a
> short rotating nudge every turn, mechanical guards (no AI commit self-credit, no silent
> force-push, command-delegation, edit-delegation via edit-guard, anti-fork-bomb, anti-speculation, task-list discipline via
> tasklist-guard), an always-on output-presentation rule (rule K: present for scannability),
> an always-on scope & fidelity discipline (simplest sufficient solution; intent over letter;
> confirm before expanding scope; match rigor to blast radius; finish what was asked / drop
> nothing), a TTL'd user-override escape hatch (~/.anti-hall/skip.json), Claude workflow skills plus Codex-native skill equivalents,
> a live two-line statusline, an opt-in mcp-reaper companion (macOS + Linux) that kills
> orphaned MCP-server processes, and a second opt-in, OPTIONAL companion (dormant unless
> DevSwarm is in use, feature-gated like the OMC integration) that detects a wedged
> DevSwarm workspace session and pokes/escalates it (never kills — a separate on-demand
> CLI is the only path that ever kills). All hooks are pure Node.js (built-ins only) and run on macOS,
> Linux, and Windows; the one prerequisite is Node >= 18 on PATH.

anti-hall targets four failure modes of coding assistants: eagerness (acting before
investigating), hallucination (stating unverified facts as truth), fix-before-diagnosis
(fixing before proving root cause), and fake completion (claiming done without running
the check). It keeps the discipline alive across long sessions with a layered defense:
protocol injection, per-turn nudges, and mechanical hooks that cannot be argued with.

## Install

```
/plugin marketplace add talas9/anti-hall
/plugin install anti-hall@anti-hall
```

Codex project-local activation:

```
node plugins/anti-hall/codex/install-codex.js
```

Codex global activation:

```
node plugins/anti-hall/codex/install-codex.js --global
```

Requires Node.js >= 18 on PATH; without it the hooks silently no-op. Updates via the
`/plugin` manager; updates pull on restart if autoUpdate is enabled (or via the /plugin manager).

## Overview

- [README](README.md): overview, the four failure modes, two-layer design, statusline, AFK mode, quickstart.
- [Plugin README](plugins/anti-hall/README.md): full component reference, how-it-works, configuration, troubleshooting, contributing.
- [Codex README](plugins/anti-hall/codex/README.md): Codex hook-subset installer, skills, OMX mapping, and parity boundaries.
- [docs/KB-codex-platform-hooks-plugins.md](docs/KB-codex-platform-hooks-plugins.md): Codex hooks, plugins, skills, MCP, AGENTS.md, config, permissions, and marketplace structure.
- [docs/KB-codex-workflow-orchestration.md](docs/KB-codex-workflow-orchestration.md): Codex workflow/swarm equivalents: subagents, worktrees, cloud/noninteractive/SDK/GitHub Action, and OMX mapping.
- [docs/KB-omx.md](docs/KB-omx.md): oh-my-codex (OMX) as the Codex-side OMC equivalent.
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md): version history (current: see plugin.json).
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): prose mirror of the verify-first Iron Law for Codex / cross-tool agents (repo root; not bundled by /plugin install).
- [docs/KB-claude-codex.md](docs/KB-claude-codex.md): knowledge base on Claude Code hook contracts, prompting, and verified Claude Code internals (cmux subagent detection, macOS memory, statusline architecture).
- [docs/KB.md](docs/KB.md): plugin knowledge base index (hooks, skills, design decisions).
- [docs/KB-claude-workflow-orchestration.md](docs/KB-claude-workflow-orchestration.md): when/how to use programmatic multi-agent orchestration (the Workflow tool) vs a single/shallow agent — patterns, cost numbers, decision criteria; 14 sources (8 official Anthropic).
- [docs/KB-codex-vs-opus-coding.md](docs/KB-codex-vs-opus-coding.md): Codex (GPT-5.5) vs Claude Opus 4.8 for coding — benchmark consensus, what each is best at, the "Codex=apply/Opus=think" framing (partial), and a recommended division of labor; 13 sources (Feb–Jun 2026).
- [docs/KB-sonnet-5.md](docs/KB-sonnet-5.md): Claude Sonnet 5 + model routing — benchmark tables (Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 5 / Haiku 4.5) AND the parallel Codex table (migrated to gpt-5.6-sol / gpt-5.6-terra / gpt-5.4-mini per the 2026-07-09 GPT-5.6 GA — see KB-gpt-5.6.md), effort behavior, pricing, task→model decision matrix, switch thresholds, the anti-hall seat routing, and cross-platform equivalence (gpt-5.6-sol↔Opus, gpt-5.6-terra↔Sonnet 5, gpt-5.4-mini/gpt-5.6-luna↔Haiku); 17 sources (2 official Anthropic + 3 official OpenAI).
- [docs/KB-gpt-5.6.md](docs/KB-gpt-5.6.md): GPT-5.6 (Sol/Terra/Luna) — OpenAI's three-tier GPT-5.6 model family: pricing, API/Codex CLI availability, this machine's local Codex CLI picker gap (upstream issue #31873), third-party coding benchmarks, confidence-labeled (PRIMARY/SECONDARY/PRESS) sourcing; 26 sources (6 OpenAI docs pages primary-fetched, 3 OpenAI announcement pages 403'd and corroborated via secondary sources).
- [docs/KB-token-usage-models.md](docs/KB-token-usage-models.md): token usage & cost mechanics — how tokens actually bill/consume, not benchmarks: thinking/reasoning-token billing (output-rate, confirmed both platforms), the full effort taxonomy (5 Claude tiers incl. Haiku 4.5's total non-support; 5 Codex tiers with a minimal/none naming inconsistency), tokenizer effects (Claude's ~30% inflation vs Codex's inherited o200k_base efficiency gain), Workflow/multi-agent cost multipliers (Anthropic's own 15×/7×/4× figures), ultracode's official definition (xhigh + auto-workflow, compounding per-task), Codex's 272K-token long-context premium, and first-party anti-hall session telemetry; 28 sources (14 official).
- [docs/KB-model-modes.md](docs/KB-model-modes.md): model operating modes — Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 5 / Haiku 4.5 effort levels + adaptive vs legacy thinking; Claude Code Plan Mode mechanics; the Workflow tool + "ultracode" (honesty-checked as officially documented, not rumored); `/code-review ultra` (ultrareview) billing/GitHub requirement; Codex/GPT-5.x reasoning-effort tiers; Codex CLI approval/sandbox modes; anti-hall routing implications including a real Auditor/Critic prompt-text terminology drift found against `ship-it.workflow.js`; 47 sources (19 official Anthropic + 17 official OpenAI).
- [docs/KB-false-completion.md](docs/KB-false-completion.md): reward hacking / specification gaming research (Anthropic, OpenAI/Apollo, METR, NIST CAISI) showing agents can fake task success; the measured claimed-vs-verified success gap on coding benchmarks (METR ≥16% disqualified-cheating rate; a 66-98pt self-report-vs-verified gap on codebase conversion); verification-before-completion mitigation patterns (external tool-grounded checks beat self-critique, which can backfire); anti-hall implications confirming ship-it v2's `STATE.json` protocol is prose-only and mechanically unenforced — a real, evidenced gap; 21 sources (8 official-vendor + 10 academic + 3 practitioner).
- [docs/KB-overengineering.md](docs/KB-overengineering.md): overengineering — general software-engineering causes (YAGNI, essential vs accidental complexity, worse-is-better, premature abstraction/optimization); AI/LLM-coding-agent-specific causes (RLHF length-bias reward hacking, benchmark misalignment, vendor counter-guidance); empirical bloat measurement (SlopCodeBench, real-world GitHub data, GitClear/DORA industry data); anti-hall implications for the SCOPE & FIDELITY discipline, `ship-it`'s S/M/L tiering, and the `debt`/`simplify` skills; 15 sources (2 official vendor + 13 academic/blog/community).
- [docs/KB-goal-setting.md](docs/KB-goal-setting.md): goal setting — classical theory (Locke & Latham goal-setting theory, SMART criteria, agile Definition of Done, OKRs) on why specific/measurable goals beat vague ones; AI-agent goal misspecification as a root cause of reward hacking/specification gaming (DeepMind, 2x Anthropic, METR, OpenAI CoastRunners); cross-vendor task-specification practice (Claude Code + Codex "Done when"/acceptance-criteria prompting guidance); anti-hall implications validating `ship-it`'s per-phase `acceptance:` field and finding a concrete goal-drift gap between Step 1's intent and Step 2's phase decomposition; 15 sources (11 official + 3 community + 1 academic).
- [docs/KB-cmux.md](docs/KB-cmux.md): cmux — the native macOS terminal for running AI coding agents in parallel (manaflow-ai, libghostty + socket CLI); disambiguates the separate craigsc/cmux worktree CLI; cmux+Claude Code notification wiring and memory/orphan gotchas; 11 sources (4 official).
- [docs/KB-omc.md](docs/KB-omc.md): oh-my-claudecode (OMC) — multi-agent orchestration layer for Claude Code (plugin marketplace, skills, agents, hooks, state); canonical launch, model routing, key skills (/team, /ralph, /autopilot, /ultrawork, /ccg); the combined cmux+OMC+Claude Code stack; agnostic.
- [docs/KB-devswarm-hivecontrol.md](docs/KB-devswarm-hivecontrol.md): DevSwarm — the multi-workspace AI IDE (each workspace = an isolated git worktree + its own agent) and its bundled `hivecontrol` CLI (v2.3.3, verified by local execution though undocumented publicly): full command surface (workspace/repo/health/open, 13+9 subcommands), `.devswarm/config.json` zod schema, the `DEVSWARM_*` env + `DEVSWARM_SOURCE_BRANCH`-empty=Primary role-detection signal, async message-passing coordination (create→monitor→check-merge→merge-into-source), and a 2-tier anti-hall orchestration integration design (L1 Primary→child workspaces via hivecontrol; L2 child→Workflow/subagents, no child-child; OMC+OMX parity); 20 sources (15 official) + 5 primary local-evidence sources (role signal confirmed by a live child-workspace probe).
- [docs/CONTEXT-PRESERVATION-KB.md](docs/CONTEXT-PRESERVATION-KB.md): swarm-researched context-discipline KB (findings-externalization, compaction cadence, context-rot sweet spot).
- [docs/TASK-WORK.md](docs/TASK-WORK.md): task-list + per-session progress-file work-tracking discipline.
- [docs/TASKLIST-GUARD.md](docs/TASKLIST-GUARD.md): tasklist-guard usage, thresholds, and escape hatch.
- [docs/E2E-TESTING.md](docs/E2E-TESTING.md): the zero-dependency `node:test` E2E suite and how to run it.

## Hooks (plugins/anti-hall/hooks/)

All pure Node, fail-open. Registered in hooks.json.

- [verify-first-full.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/verify-first-full.js): SessionStart — full Iron-Law + rationalization-table protocol, the always-on scope & fidelity discipline (simplest sufficient solution; intent over letter; confirm before expanding scope; match rigor to blast radius; finish what was asked / drop nothing), and skill primer; re-fires after compaction (source=compact).
- [verify-first-subagent.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/verify-first-subagent.js): SubagentStart — re-injects the Iron Law + rationalization table + positive rules + scope-fidelity into each spawned subagent; deliberately omits the orchestration/delegate block (subagents are workers; re-injecting it would recreate deep nesting). Shared core in verify-first-core.js.
- [verify-first-core.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/verify-first-core.js): Shared module (not a hook) — single source of truth for the Iron Law content shared by verify-first-full.js and verify-first-subagent.js; prevents drift between the two hooks.
- [version-alert.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/version-alert.js): SessionStart (non-blocking) — alerts when a newer anti-hall version is available; reads running version vs cached latest (~/.anti-hall/version-check.json); emits a one-line nudge if behind; cache refresh via detached unref'd version-alert-refresh.js (no sync network at SessionStart); off-switch ANTIHALL_VERSION_ALERT=off; skip-guard hatch.
- [fable-availability.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/fable-availability.js): SessionStart (non-blocking) — reads ~/.claude.json's modelAccessCache/additionalModelOptionsCache (the same cache /model renders from) once per session to detect Fable 5 availability; no live API probe, fail-open, silent unless available. When available, threads args.fableAvailable=true into ship-it/deadly-loop Workflow invocations; Reviewer seat fallback chain extends to Fable 5 → Sonnet 5 → Opus.
- [verify-first.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/verify-first.js): UserPromptSubmit — short varying one-line nudge.
- [git-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/git-guard.js): PreToolUse Bash — quote-aware tokenizer blocks AI self-credit attribution in git commit trailers AND in `gh pr/issue/release create|edit|comment` --body/--title (🤖 footer, Co-Authored-By, claude.com/claude-code link; inline values only, --body-file fail-open), plus git push --force.
- [api-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/api-guard.js): PreToolUse Write/Edit/MultiEdit — the mechanical answer to API hallucination: resolves module.attribute references in code-to-be-written against the installed python3/node and BLOCKS the write when a real module is missing the attribute. Default scope = stdlib/builtins (import-safe, no untrusted code); opt-in ANTIHALL_API_GUARD_THIRDPARTY=1 also verifies installed 3rd-party packages (off by default — a deadly-loop proved that verifying a package = importing it = running its code at edit time). Never probes local/relative modules; 0 FP + full in-scope catch on eval/api-guard-bench.js; fail-open.
- [command-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/command-guard.js): PreToolUse Bash — blocks heavy commands in the COORDINATOR (pushes them to subagents); subagent-aware via the hook payload (works under cmux where the entrypoint env is unreliable). Under a DevSwarm-active session it also redirects destructive native inbox reads in ALL contexts (own skip `devswarm-read-guard`): `hivecontrol workspace monitor` AND `read-messages` both block UNCONDITIONALLY (no evidence check — a raw `read-messages` desyncs the durable cursor regardless of whether a durable inbox exists); also blocks a raw shell read (`cat`/`head`/`grep`/`sed`/`awk`/…) of the durable inbox/store files via `hooks/lib/devswarm-inbox-paths.js`'s shared classifier (redirects to `devswarm.js inbox pull/read/messages`); quoted DATA / smuggled `bash -c`/`$()` handled like the heavy path; fail-open.
- [inbox-read-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/inbox-read-guard.js): PreToolUse Read, OPTIONAL/feature-gated, **Claude-only** (not registered on the Codex port — it guards Claude's own `Read` tool specifically; the Bash-side raw-read block above is shared and fires on Codex too). Blocks a direct `Read`-tool open of a raw DevSwarm inbox/store file, using the same `devswarm-inbox-paths.js` classifier as `command-guard.js`: `inbox/**` denied unconditionally, the store db/journal denied once a Primary read path exists to serve the same data (`inbox messages` — shipped), everything else under the DevSwarm root (`summary.json`, `cursors/**`, `workspaces/**`, `liveness/**`, `heartbeats/**`, `locks/**`) allowed. Fail-open; own `devswarm-read-guard` skip name.
- [edit-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/edit-guard.js): PreToolUse Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit — blocks a COORDINATOR from editing files directly, requiring delegation to a subagent (subagents always pass); DevSwarm-aware block wording. Root-anchored allowlist (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md, .claude/**, .omc/**, .anti-hall/**, root-only PLAN.md/STATE.json), extensible via ANTIHALL_EDIT_GUARD_ALLOW. Skip-guard hatch: edit-guard (not in the destructive set). Shares detection with command-guard.js via coordinator-detect.js. Fail-open.
- [coordinator-detect.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/coordinator-detect.js): shared module (not a hook, not registered in hooks.json) — the single coordinator-vs-subagent discriminator, extracted from command-guard.js and consumed by command-guard.js, edit-guard.js, and graphify-guard.js.
- [codex-availability.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/codex-availability.js): SessionStart — OS-agnostic PATH probe (Windows PATHEXT-aware) for a real codex executable; writes ~/.anti-hall/codex-availability.json once per session so coordinators/skills read the cached fact instead of re-probing. Proves reachability only, not authentication/readiness. Registered on both the Claude plugin and the Codex port. Fail-open.
- [model-routing-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/model-routing-guard.js): PreToolUse Agent/Task — anti-waste routing: classifies spawn descriptions by keyword signals (mechanical vs complex) and nudges toward the cheapest fitting model. Advisory on explicit flagship + mechanical task. **Strict is now the default (v0.35.0+):** omitted-model mechanical spawns are blocked unconditionally (set ANTIHALL_MODEL_ROUTING=advisory to opt out and revert to advisory-only). Debate role-words in the spawn description (reviewer/auditor/critic/debate/deadly-loop) downgrade a row-1 block to advisory. Fail-open; never blocks unknown model tokens.
- [omc-detect.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/omc-detect.js): shared helper (not a hook) — detects whether an oh-my-claudecode autonomous loop (ralph, ultrawork, autopilot, ultraqa, team, ultrapilot, pipeline, omc-teams) is currently active and fresh. Consumed by task-guard and tasklist-guard to suppress Stop-blocks (emit advisory instead) when an OMC loop is running — prevents deadlock against the loop. Fail-open direction = NOT deferring (false): missing/malformed state = not active. Kill-switches: DISABLE_OMC=1 or OMC_SKIP_HOOKS including 'persistent-mode'.
- [swarm-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/swarm-guard.js): PreToolUse Agent/Task — anti-fork-bomb: spawn-rate cap + real available-memory check (vm_stat / MemAvailable, not os.freemem()). A blocked spawn also logs one line to ~/.anti-hall/swarm-trips.log (observation only — doesn't feed the rate window).
- [phase-tracker.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/phase-tracker.js): PreToolUse Agent/Task — records subagent spawns to a homedir log so the statusline shows live swarm activity automatically. It also writes a rolling `~/.anti-hall/agents/recent-spawn.json` heartbeat that `agentsRunning()` consumes, so the Stop guards know when parallel work is live. Never blocks.
- [agent-watchdog.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/agent-watchdog.js): CLI heartbeat helper (NOT a hook) — scans `~/.anti-hall/agents/*.json` heartbeats and reports stale/hung subagents; run manually by the orchestration skill.
- [task-tracker.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/task-tracker.js): UserPromptSubmit — task-list discipline directive (+ a one-line freshness note when open/stale tasks exist).
- [limit-conserve-inject.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/limit-conserve-inject.js): UserPromptSubmit — limit-conservation mode: injects a token-conservation nudge when context usage reaches ANTIHALL_LIMIT_THRESHOLD (default 85%). ANTIHALL_LIMIT_CONSERVE: auto (default, reads OMC usage cache) / on / off. Auto requires OMC; without it, manual on/off only. Account-aware: deactivates if the logged-in Claude account changes before the usage cache refreshes under it (kill-switch ANTIHALL_LIMIT_ACCOUNT_CHECK=off). Skip-guard hatch: limit-conserve.
- [limit-conserve.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/limit-conserve.js): shared helper (not a hook) — reads the OMC usage cache and applies threshold logic; consumed by limit-conserve-inject.js. Tracks the current Claude account's userID (~/.claude.json) alongside the usage cache's mtime so a post-switch stale reading from the old account is never mis-applied.
- [task-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/task-guard.js): Stop (loop-safe) — blocks once when the session ends with genuinely-open tasks.
- [tasklist-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/tasklist-guard.js): Stop — enforces a live task-list + fresh per-session progress file (`.anti-hall/progress/<date>/<session-id>.md`) for non-trivial work (≥ threshold file-mutating actions); coexists with task-guard with its own independent block cap.
- [skip-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/skip-guard.js): shared user-override escape-hatch primitive read by the guards — a TTL'd, granular `~/.anti-hall/skip.json` marker; a broad `all` skip excludes the destructive git-guard (it must be named explicitly).
- [speculation-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/speculation-guard.js): Stop — flags hedge-word speculation; confident inference-as-fact is the opt-in speculation-judge (Tier 3).
- [speculation-judge.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/speculation-judge.js): Stop — opt-in LLM-evaluated speculation check (off by default).
- [codex-nudge.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/codex-nudge.js): Stop (advisory) — nudges once/session for an independent Codex second-opinion review when substantial code shipped with no Codex review; off-switch ANTIHALL_CODEX_NUDGE=off.
- [ship-it-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/ship-it-guard.js): PreToolUse Write/Edit/MultiEdit — OPT-IN, default OFF (the only opt-in code-edit gate). With ANTIHALL_SHIPIT_GATE ∈ {1,true,yes,on}, blocks a CODE edit on a hard-risk path (migration/auth/.github/workflows/security) when no PLAN.md exists (repo root). Also does a conformance advisory (never blocks) for edits outside a PLAN.md's declared files:. Enforces artifact existence only, conservative, fail-open; no effect when unset.
- [merge-gate.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/merge-gate.js): PreToolUse Bash — OPT-IN, default OFF (a backstop, not a guarantee). With ANTIHALL_MERGE_GATE ∈ {1,true,yes,on}, blocks an auto-merge (gh pr merge incl. --auto, gh pr review --approve, git merge --no-ff/--ff into main/master/develop, and hivecontrol workspace merge-into-source/merge-from-source) when the agent's own recent output carries an unresolved self-hedge not followed by a resolution token. Keyword-heuristic, bypassable, fail-open, cannot hard-loop; no effect when unset.
- [graphify-session.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/graphify-session.js) / [graphify-reminder.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/graphify-reminder.js) / [graphify-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/graphify-guard.js): query-the-graph-first priming, update reminder, and search guard (no-op without a graph).
- [hooks.json](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/hooks.json): hook registration manifest.

## Skills (plugins/anti-hall/skills/)

Invoke as /anti-hall:<name>.

- [root-cause](plugins/anti-hall/skills/root-cause/SKILL.md): evidence-driven debugging to the original + root cause (also enforced always-on as a discipline).
- [orchestration](plugins/anti-hall/skills/orchestration/SKILL.md): non-blocking swarm; coordinator/worker hierarchy; watchdog + heartbeat; live phase statusline; verify delegated work — a subagent's "done/passing" is an unverified claim re-checked against ground truth before marking complete; defaults delegated heavy/parallel work to the background (coordinator passes run_in_background so the user needn't background it manually) while still verifying each on completion (also always-on).
- [deadly-loop](plugins/anti-hall/skills/deadly-loop/SKILL.md): iterative Reviewer + Critic debate + fix-waves until zero new P0s or P1s. On convergence, writes an advisory ~/.anti-hall/approvals/<repo>@<sha>.json record ("proof": false — not authorization; a real gate must still enforce its own check).
- [ship-it](plugins/anti-hall/skills/ship-it/SKILL.md): one lean workflow for shipping any change correctly, scaled S/M/L to blast radius — brainstorm + plan IN PLAN MODE (ExitPlanMode is the approval gate), harden the plan with the deadly-loop BEFORE code, fan large work out as a Workflow swarm, verify each phase with fresh evidence + a vacuous-test guard until zero NEW P0s or P1s. Replaces feature-launch. L tier: resumable STATE.json (plan hash + per-phase status + escalation cap of 2), P2 findings logged to decisions.md, Codex-primary/Sonnet-5-failover build seats with a cross-model no-self-review guard, and an end-of-run session-history entry + SUMMARY.md + `graphify update .`.
- [install-statusline](plugins/anti-hall/skills/install-statusline/SKILL.md): installs the statusLine entry on demand (global by default, per-project on request) + restart reminder. --consolidate merges with an existing statusline (e.g., OMC HUD) instead of replacing it; base persisted to ~/.anti-hall/consolidated-base.json. Env: ANTIHALL_STATUSLINE_BASE pins the base expression explicitly.
- [doctor](plugins/anti-hall/skills/doctor/SKILL.md): health-check AND repair — confirms Node is found, every hook is present + syntax-valid, and the guards actually fire (live behavioral self-tests). Env-aware: also detects + tests OMC, Codex/OMX, and the DevSwarm liveness supervisor (installed-companion state + per-workspace liveness self-test; `nudged` reads as WARN not FAIL), silent and skipped when not present. Repair mode (v0.55.0): plain `doctor` auto-applies AUTO-SAFE fixes (state migrations, statusline-if-missing, idempotent supervisor/codex refresh) and GATED daemon fixes (ingest install / wrong-path rebind / stale-script) only under the DevSwarm gate; `--check` = pure read-only (CI), `--dry-run` = preview, `--fix`/`--repair` = explicit aliases.
- [update](plugins/anti-hall/skills/update/SKILL.md): in-session self-update — git pull --ff-only, semver-anchored traversal-proof cache sync, changelog delta, /reload-plugins in-session reload. After pulling, also runs scripts/migrate-state.js once per repo (idempotent) to fold legacy root .anti-hall-progress.md / .anti-hall-history.md into .anti-hall/history/legacy/, then scripts/capability-scan.js (read-only) to report each opt-in capability this build ships as available-vs-active on this machine, with the exact command to close any gap.
- [devswarm](plugins/anti-hall/skills/devswarm/SKILL.md): explains anti-hall's optional DevSwarm integration — the hivecontrol reference KB, the designed-but-unbuilt workspace-tier orchestration, the shipped layered recovery model (child self-report → supervisor poke → escalate — automatic path never kills), and the on-demand `devswarm-recover` CLI (the only path that ever kills), plus the full activation checklist + tunable env vars.
- [system-briefing](plugins/anti-hall/skills/system-briefing/SKILL.md): the orientation companion to `doctor` — a DERIVED (never hardcoded) live inventory for the agent that installs/operates anti-hall. Runs `scripts/briefing.js`, which reads the real `hooks.json` + the files on disk and enumerates every hook grouped by event (each with its own one-line purpose), the shipped skills, the DevSwarm coordination substrate (the 4 mechanical triggers · store · CLI · auto-safe migration · supervisor), and a docs/KB map — so it can't drift. `doctor` answers "do the guards fire?"; `system-briefing` answers "what IS this build and how is it wired?". Codex mirror: `codex/skills/anti-hall-system-briefing`.
- [deadly-loop-multi](plugins/anti-hall/skills/deadly-loop-multi/SKILL.md): scaled-up deadly-loop — N parallel Reviewer + Critic pairs with diversified lenses, then deduplicate + synthesize (double / triple / quadruple).
- [flutter-debug](plugins/anti-hall/skills/flutter-debug/SKILL.md): agent-driven Flutter debug loop (run + hot reload + visually verified UI changes); reproduces bugs → reads errors (exceptions / layout / logs / VM service) → roots cause → fixes → re-verifies with screenshots. iOS fully; Android run/reload/errors today, taps/screenshots pending FP7 probe. Zero-setup MCP + app-side marionette integration (scope-aware `claude mcp add`, auto-apply dependencies). Delegates to the `flutter-debug` agent (plugins/anti-hall/agents/flutter-debug.md, the plugin's first shipped agent).
- [activate](plugins/anti-hall/skills/activate/SKILL.md): one-shot idempotent first-run setup (user-invoked only, never auto-run). Checks & installs the statusline (user scope by default; offers project-scope on conflict), reports model-routing state (strict by default), writes ~/.anti-hall/activated.json sentinel, prints restart reminder if settings changed. Reuses install-statusline.js — no reimplemented logic.
- [simplify](plugins/anti-hall/skills/simplify/SKILL.md): behavior-preserving simplification harvest on recently-changed (or named) code. Tags each finding delete:/stdlib:/native:/yagni:/shrink:/slop:, applies the safe set, re-runs the tests, and reports a single MEASURED `net: -N lines` score (the real post-apply diff delta, never a projected estimate — verify-first rule 10). Declines anything that removes capability (scope change ≠ simplification).
- [debt](plugins/anti-hall/skills/debt/SKILL.md): register + auditor for DELIBERATE technical debt via `// anti-hall: <ceiling>,<when>` markers (a budgeted, harvestable alternative to vague TODOs). Greps the tree (scripts/harvest-debt.js, pure Node, comment-syntax-agnostic), parses each ceiling + payback trigger, flags rot-risk when a marker has no trigger or sits in code untouched past a staleness threshold (git-based; fail-open when git absent). Not a license to skip real work.
- [MODEL-POLICY.md](plugins/anti-hall/skills/MODEL-POLICY.md): shared TRIO debate roster (Reviewer = Sonnet 5 `model:"sonnet"`; Auditor = latest Opus `model:"opus"`, divergent regression/coupling lens; Critic = latest OpenAI Codex at `xhigh` reasoning, Opus adversarial-persona fallback). **Duplicated** across two locations (skills/MODEL-POLICY.md + skills/deadly-loop/references/MODEL-POLICY.md) — keep both copies byte-identical; symlinks are stripped on install.

## Agents (plugins/anti-hall/agents/)

Auto-discovered by Claude Code; the flutter-debug agent is the plugin's first shipped agent.

- [flutter-debug.md](plugins/anti-hall/agents/flutter-debug.md): executor persona for Flutter app debugging (run + hot reload + visually verified fixes). Carries the full debug loop protocol so direct spawns work without the skill. Model: sonnet (code-authoring floor). Escalates to opus on complex redesigns or after 2 iterations without root cause.

## Statusline (plugins/anti-hall/statusline/)

Two-line statusline the plugin renders itself.

- Line 1 (rich): project name, git (branch/worktree/stash/status/ahead-behind), model, effort, subagents, duration, context %, cost.
- Line 2 (always-on, 3 tiers): coordinator-set phase progress bar > auto "orchestrating · N agents" activity bar (from phase-tracker) > idle context-window gauge.
- [STATUSLINE.md](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/STATUSLINE.md): install + revert.
- [statusline.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/statusline.js): dispatcher (line 1 = base wrap or own-dispatch rich renderer; line 2 = phase-bar.js).
- [statusline-rich.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/statusline-rich.js): the rich line-1 renderer.
- [phase-bar.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/phase-bar.js): the 3-tier line-2 renderer.
- [phase.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/phase.js): the orchestrator's progress writer (set/advance/step/agents/clear).
- [install-statusline.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/install-statusline.js) / [uninstall-statusline.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/uninstall-statusline.js): installer / uninstaller.

## Companion (plugins/anti-hall/companion/)

Opt-in, NOT a hook. macOS + Linux interval job; Windows is a documented no-op (no parent-death reparenting + PID recycling make external orphan detection unsafe — the correct fix there is Job Objects set by the spawner).

- [mcp-reaper.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/mcp-reaper.js): kills ONLY orphaned MCP-server processes (parent already died). Safety invariant: reaps a process only if it matches a generic MCP signature AND its parent is a reaper/init (launchd / init / `systemd --user`); since Unix reparents a dead process's children, a live MCP always has a live spawner as parent, so killing an in-use server is impossible by construction. Recognizes Python MCPs too (uvx/uv + underscore mcp_server_* forms). Limitation: an MCP run as a LaunchAgent / `systemd --user` unit / OS service shares init as a parent (like a leaked orphan) and can be reaped — exclude it via ANTIHALL_REAPER_EXCLUDE='name|name'. Env: MCP_REAP_DRYRUN=1, MCP_REAP_GRACE, ANTIHALL_REAPER_MATCH, ANTIHALL_REAPER_EXCLUDE.
- [install-reaper.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/install-reaper.js): `node install-reaper.js` installs (macOS LaunchAgent / Linux systemd --user timer, cron fallback); `--uninstall` removes; `--dry-run` previews.
- [companion/README.md](plugins/anti-hall/companion/README.md): full reference + safety rationale.
- [devswarm-supervisor.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/devswarm-supervisor.js) (+ [install-devswarm-supervisor.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/install-devswarm-supervisor.js)): a second opt-in, OPTIONAL companion — dormant with zero effect unless DevSwarm is in use (feature-gated via `hooks/lib/devswarm-detect.js`, modeled on `omc-detect.js`). Detects a wedged/idle DevSwarm workspace agent from outbound activity (transcript + git/worktree) and pokes it (an optional descriptor `nudgeCommand`) or escalates (log + optional `escalateCommand`) — **never kills**. `node install-devswarm-supervisor.js` installs (macOS LaunchAgent / Linux systemd --user timer); `--uninstall` removes. macOS + Linux full; Windows detection-only. Workaround for claude-code#39755. Sweep thresholds are env-tunable (seconds; clamped, invalid/absent falls back to the default): ANTIHALL_DEVSWARM_IDLE_SEC (900), ANTIHALL_DEVSWARM_COOLDOWN_SEC (600), ANTIHALL_DEVSWARM_NUDGE_MAX_ATTEMPTS (2), ANTIHALL_DEVSWARM_NUDGE_WINDOW_SEC (180), ANTIHALL_DEVSWARM_NUDGE_COOLDOWN_SEC (120).
- [devswarm-recover.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/devswarm-recover.js): the ONLY path in DevSwarm that ever kills a process — an on-demand CLI (`node devswarm-recover.js <workspace-id>`), invoked explicitly per workspace (e.g. on an `escalated` verdict). Resolves the one confirmed wedged `claude` target (identity-bound, abstains on ambiguity, TOCTOU-reconfirmed before each signal, group-killed) and resumes it headless (`claude --resume`); unlike the automatic supervisor it also targets an interactive session (naming the id is the deliberate override). Windows: escalate-only. Env: ANTIHALL_DEVSWARM_MAX_RECOVERIES (3), ANTIHALL_DEVSWARM_GRACE_SEC (5).
- [devswarm-child-role.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/devswarm-child-role.js): SessionStart hook, OPTIONAL/feature-gated — Layer 1 of the layered recovery model. For a DevSwarm CHILD workspace only (`hooks/lib/devswarm-role.js`'s `isChildWorkspace` + `devswarm-detect.js` active), injects a reminder to proactively self-report idleness (`hivecontrol workspace message-parent`) rather than sit unnoticed. Silent no-op for Primary/non-DevSwarm sessions.
- [devswarm-parent-inbox.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/devswarm-parent-inbox.js): UserPromptSubmit hook, OPTIONAL/feature-gated, PRIMARY only — the mechanical trigger for the "Primary neglects child workspaces" failure (claude-code#39755). Each turn surfaces the real unread/idle state of active workspaces so the Primary engages them, and recommends archiving any workspace the store derived as complete (`archive_ready`). Reads the durable-inbox files + the supervisor's already-written verdicts + `summary.json`; never runs `computeLiveness`/git on the hot path. As of 0.54.1, also injects a compact live status table EVERY turn (one row per active workspace: workspace/status [`escalated`>`stale`>`archive-ready`>`active`, attention first]/finishing-rate [required gates met/total + optional heartbeat %]/unread/last-activity; capped at 12 rows, `+N more` logged not silently dropped). Silent no-op for child/non-DevSwarm sessions.
- [devswarm-parent-gate.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/devswarm-parent-gate.js): Stop hook, OPTIONAL/feature-gated, PRIMARY only, capped/loop-safe — blocks the Primary from ending its turn while a child still has unread backlog past its cursor OR the supervisor already judged a child stale/escalated. Reads only files (the fs cursor via `companion/lib/devswarm-inbox-cursor.js` + the supervisor's verdict file) — no git, no live liveness on the ~30s Stop path. Fail-open.
- [devswarm-child-turn.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/devswarm-child-turn.js): UserPromptSubmit hook, OPTIONAL/feature-gated, CHILD only — writes a turn-authored heartbeat (`~/.anti-hall/devswarm/heartbeats/<branch>.json`, authored by the turn itself, NEVER a background ticker) and reminds the child to report to its parent. Also runs a non-destructive unread check against the child's OWN durable descriptor inbox and, when >0, surfaces the count + the safe `inbox read` path; nudges the child to run `devswarm.js inbox pull` (SHIPPED as of 0.54.2 — `companion/lib/devswarm-pull.js`'s bounded `message-count`-gated drain) to actually fill that inbox from the native queue. Silent no-op otherwise.
- [devswarm-child-gate.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/devswarm-child-gate.js): Stop hook, OPTIONAL/feature-gated, CHILD only, capped/loop-safe — forces the child to emit a heartbeat / self-report to its parent (`hivecontrol workspace message-parent`) before going idle, so a child that finishes a turn pings the parent instead of dropping off its radar and later reading as stale. As of 0.54.1, stays silent (no forced ack) when the child's own turn-authored heartbeat is already fresh (<5 min) — the over-nag fix, avoiding a duplicate report on every Stop. Fail-open.
- [devswarm-store.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/lib/devswarm-store.js): the persistent WRITE/DERIVE side of the DevSwarm substrate — ONE API, TWO interchangeable backends chosen by FEATURE-DETECT (`try { require('node:sqlite') }` → WAL sqlite, else an append-only NDJSON journal; dependency-free, green on Node 18/20 through 22/24). Hooks NEVER open the DB: it derives a `summary.json` projection (atomic tmp+rename) that hooks read. Tables: messages (timestamped, append-only, idempotent by dedupe hash), registry, cursors, gates (per-workspace named completion gates, timestamped + append-only). Derives `archive_ready: true` when ALL required gates are met for a still-present workspace (required set configurable, default `done,merged,tests_passed` via `ANTIHALL_DEVSWARM_REQUIRED_GATES`); agnostic about what any consumer gate means.
- [devswarm.js](plugins/anti-hall/scripts/devswarm.js): THE structured CLI (CLI over MCP, owner preference) — stable JSON on stdout, pure Node built-ins. Subcommands: register/ensure (write a workspace descriptor + populate sessionId), register-primary (register the CURRENT worktree's Primary under its per-worktree id `primary-<hash>`, never a shared `'primary'`), heartbeat (turn-authored), inbox pull (child-side bounded native-queue drain into the durable inbox), inbox count/read/ack (the durable-inbox cursor primitive; ack advances the cursor = the parent-gate's non-skip clear path), inbox messages/read-primary (Primary/store non-destructive read of message bodies straight from the store — no descriptor needed, never touches the native queue; `--unread`/`--ack` against a separate `cursors/<id>.json` ACK cursor), workspaces list (derive+emit summary.json), gate --set/--clear (mark completion gates), nudge (poke-or-escalate), archive (archive-by-absence on anti-hall's OWN registry — hivecontrol has NO teardown command, so it SURFACES a manual "remove workspace in the DevSwarm app" step and never deletes), archive-ignore/archive-unignore, migrate. Full reference with source-line citations: `docs/KB-devswarm-hivecontrol.md` §8.8. `command-guard.js` carries a root-anchored LIGHT_EXCEPTION for it so the guard doesn't block its own wrapper.
- [devswarm-migrate.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/devswarm-migrate.js) (+ [devswarm-ingest.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/devswarm-ingest.js)): the AUTO-SAFE migration + ingest daemon. `migrate` dual-reads existing on-disk state (the JSON registry descriptors + each descriptor's legacy NDJSON inbox/cursor) into the store — IDEMPOTENT (dedupe hash from id+line-index+content), NON-DESTRUCTIVE (reads sources only; never deletes/moves/truncates, so legacy files stay byte-for-byte and rollback is always possible), SINGLE-CONSUMER-LOCKED (O_EXCL), and COUNT-VERIFIED before it reports success. `devswarm-ingest.js` = the one supervised daemon wrapping the native `hivecontrol monitor` → store (dedupe-idempotent); refuses to start if another monitor consumer is already running (lockfile), mechanically enforcing the single-native-consumer invariant.
- [install-devswarm-ingest.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/install-devswarm-ingest.js): NEW in 0.54.1 — installs/refreshes `devswarm-ingest.js` as a CONTINUOUS supervised daemon (re-exec-on-exit, unlike the periodic supervisor sweep): macOS LaunchAgent `KeepAlive`; Linux `systemd --user` `.service` with `Restart=always` (cron fallback — every minute, restart-if-dead — when `systemctl` is absent; up to ~60s revive gap on a cron-only Linux host after a crash). **PER-PROJECT identity, not per-machine:** label/unit/lock/cron-marker/own reception workspace id (`primary-<hash>`) are derived from an 8-hex hash of the worktree it was installed FROM (`worktreeHash`/`labelForWorktree`/`unitForWorktree`/`primaryWorkspaceId`) — a second repo's install ADDS a unit rather than overwriting the first, since `hivecontrol` itself resolves a workspace by walking up from the process's cwd, not by id. Idempotent for the SAME repo (single-consumer lock refuses a redundant instance for that worktree); multi-repo coverage needs this installer run once per repo — a repo with no install of its own has zero ingest coverage. Windows: documented no-op. Auto-installed/refreshed by the `update` skill (no offer, no ask) inside an active DevSwarm session for the repo the update runs in, same posture as `install-devswarm-supervisor.js` — closes the gap where the ingest daemon shipped in code (0.54.0) but nothing started it.

## Manifests

- [plugins/anti-hall/.claude-plugin/plugin.json](plugins/anti-hall/.claude-plugin/plugin.json): plugin manifest; version is the sole authority.
- [.claude-plugin/marketplace.json](.claude-plugin/marketplace.json): marketplace manifest.

## Tests

- [tests/](tests/): zero-dependency `node --test` (node:test) E2E suite — 783 passing (+2 platform-skipped), 785 total, covering the hooks (guards, api-guard stdlib/builtin/3rd-party checks + shadowing/portability/RCE-no-execute, git-guard gh-PR self-credit, speculation, tasklist-guard Bash-regex cases + history-ledger reminder, synthesize-never-relay nudge, etc.), flutter-debug preflight, and verify-first-subagent SubagentStart re-injection. Run with `node --test`. api-guard's catch/FP bench: `node eval/api-guard-bench.js`.
- [docs/KB-fable-5.md](docs/KB-fable-5.md): Fable 5 knowledge base — identity, pricing, context window, features, tier-token routing; 14 sources, compiled 2026-06-10.
- [docs/2026-06-10-v0.32.0-fable5-model-routing-plan.md](docs/2026-06-10-v0.32.0-fable5-model-routing-plan.md): v0.32.0 design plan — Fable 5 support, model-routing guard, TRIO roster update, deadly-swarm-converged; 2026-06-10.
- [.github/workflows/test.yml](.github/workflows/test.yml): CI runs the suite on push/PR.

## Read more

Start with [README.md](README.md), then [plugins/anti-hall/README.md](plugins/anti-hall/README.md)
for depth. The design rationale and verified Claude Code internals are in
[docs/KB-claude-codex.md](docs/KB-claude-codex.md).
